key_insight: A recurrent neural network can act as a fast surrogate for hysteresis and eddy-current behavior in laminated ferromagnetic cores, preserving accuracy while greatly reducing finite-element simulation cost.
engineering_relevance: This enables more practical magnetodynamic design of electrical machines by making detailed core physics feasible in routine finite-element workflows.
method: Train an RNN on diverse synthetic magnetic field sequences representative of machine operating conditions
Embed the surrogate material model into 2D magnetodynamic FEM with magnetic vector potential
Compare against a reference laminated-core model and an anhysteretic baseline
evidence_signal: The abstract reports excellent agreement with the reference laminated-core model, with total computational cost reduced to about twice that of an anhysteretic simulation.
watch_item: Generalization depends on training coverage, so sequences outside the synthetic dataset or materially different laminations may require retraining or validation.
paper_title: Accounting for Hysteresis and Eddy Currents in Finite Element Simulations of Ferromagnetic Laminated Cores using a Recurrent Neural Network
